front: Painting: depicts men posed in front of slabs of meat; early example of genre painting in Italy

front: Artist: founded the Accademia dei Desiderosi (Academy of the Desirous) in Bologna with family members
Artist: John Ruskin called this family 'the scum of Titian'
Artist: Baroque critic Giovanni Bellori praised this painter over Caravaggio's realism
Artist: said to have invented the caricature, claiming the task was the same as for the classical artist
Artist: a member of this Bolognese family called Vasari a 'shitface' in marginal notes on the Lives
Painting: includes a depiction of Luxuria/Vice with her back to the viewer, a pose borrowed by Caravaggio for a violin-playing angel
Artist: brother Agostino created the only surviving copy of I Modi, erotic engravings by Marcantonio Raimondi
Artist: William Hogarth praised this painter's Christ and the Samaritan Woman as exemplary for illustrating attitudes of subjects
Painting: Gentileschi's version of this subject eschewed the lush garden in this painter's version, per Mary Garrard
